specific_location: Lathrop State Park. West end of Martin Lake, close to North Martin inlet.
description: Larger than the robins close by. Very rufous back, wings and and crown. Thin black and white wing bars. Long rufous tail. Yellow eye. Breast and belly thinly, but boldly streaked dark blackish brown on buffy almost white underparts. Streaks were more rufous on flanks and sides of neck. Very striking. Relatively long legged.Behaviors: Bird was seen in flight with a flock of Robins. Robins may have spooked it up. It flew across the road in front of me and lit very briefly in the open. Then it dove deep into the undergrowth.Call: Bird was not vocalizingPlumage: Adult
